title: Rocks That Look Like Elephant Skin Show Old Ocean Life
summary: A scientist named Rowan Martindale found a rock in Morocco that had wrinkles like elephant skin. She thought it looked like fossilized microbial mats, which are patterns made by tiny living things called microbes. These microbes lived over 180 million years ago in the deep ocean. Usually, scientists think such wrinkles form in shallow water where microbes use sunlight. But this rock was from deep water, where it's dark. Martindale and her team have a new idea: an underwater landslide brought food to the seafloor, helping microbes grow without sunlight. They used chemicals for energy, like some microbes do today on whale carcasses in the deep ocean. This discovery helps us learn about ancient life in dark places.

keywords.explanation: tiny living things too small to see
turned into rock over a long time
lines or folds on a surface
the dark, deep part of the sea
when dirt and rocks slide down underwater

questions.question: Who found the rock with wrinkles?
What did the wrinkles on the rock look like?
How old are the microbes in the rock?
Where was the rock found to be from?
What did the landslide bring to the seafloor?
How do some deep-ocean microbes get energy today?

background_read: This article is about a discovery in geology, the study of rocks and Earth's history. Scientists like Rowan Martindale look at rocks to learn about past life and environments. Microbes are very small organisms that can leave marks in rocks when they fossilize. The deep ocean is a dark place where life can survive without sunlight by using chemicals. This helps us understand how life existed long ago in different parts of the ocean.
Article_Structure: WHO: Rowan Martindale, a geologist, and her team
WHAT: Found wrinkled rocks that show ancient microbial life in the deep ocean
WHERE: In Morocco and the deep ocean
WHY: To learn about how microbes lived in dark places long ago

perspectives.description: Finding new evidence that changes what we know about ancient ocean life
